candidate_lesson.lesson_text: When the 30-indicator consensus shows high conviction but green_matches=0 and hist_hr=0%, treat the directional call as UNPROVEN and refuse to size up — conviction without any historical analog match is untested pattern space.
candidate_lesson.mechanism: green_matches counts how many times the current live indicator configuration matched a historically profitable memory state. When it is 0, the conviction number is measuring internal agreement among indicators, NOT evidence that this specific configuration has ever paid off. AUDNZD showing conv=100% with green_matches=0 and hist_hr=0% is a crowded indicator config that has never been validated against realized outcomes — high agreement in untested space is how systems walk confidently into novel losing regimes.
candidate_lesson.testable_rule: if consensus_conviction >= 0.60 and green_matches == 0 and hist_hr == 0.0: size_multiplier *= 0.5  # freeze up-sizing until a historical analog confirms
candidate_lesson.expected_impact: Reduces drawdown variance and protects Sharpe by preventing full-size entries on untested high-conviction configs; expected +0.02 to +0.03 Sharpe via variance reduction rather than added return.
candidate_lesson.evidence_from_harvest: Live consensus shows AUDNZD dir=SHORT conv=100% green_matches=0 hist_hr=0%, GBPUSD dir=LONG conv=67% green_matches=0 hist_hr=0%, USDJPY dir=LONG conv=42% green_matches=0 hist_hr=0% — three high/medium conviction calls all with zero historical validation, while the only config WITH a green_match (EURUSD, green_matches=1) shows hist_hr=73% despite low conv=8%.

reflection.system_health_note: Zero trades in the last 7 days with regime=UNKNOWN and forex flagged NO_TRADE_TODAY — the system is correctly frozen, so any Sharpe-gap closure this cycle must come from gating rules and sizing discipline rather than new entries.
